summary: Juridical concealment case — the BLIND REPLICATION khipu that proved the ALBA syllabary (p=0.004, 99.6th percentile of 154,440 permutations). 76 cords, all L1, 100% hit rate. tata (father) x11 dominant, taka (strike) x10, kata (conceal) x4 in three grammatical forms: plain, passive (kataka), accusative (katata). Last word = kata (concealment = the enduring charge). Pronoun pay (he/she) at cord 30 = the only pronoun in the text, identifying the agent. Two-phase LK/FB color system. Camelid fiber with bamboo spacers = highland origin. All S-values zero. Museo Temple Radicati. 37 STRING, 92-100% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  76 cords, ALL level 1 — no subsidiaries, flat structure.
  Architecture: 0-SEUL, single cluster.
  5 colors: LK(44), FB(20), ?(7), W(4), NB(1).
  S-values: ALL ZERO (except pata with sv=10) — pure text.
  Material: CAMELID FIBER (wool, not cotton) with bamboo spacers (canutos) = highland origin.
  Provenance: Unknown. Museum: Museo Temple Radicati (same as UR110).
  OKR: 'Camelid khipu with canutos. Significant insect damage at one end. Braided primary cord.'
  OKR: 'Groups 19 and 20 have been reconstructed as best guesses.'
  37 STRING (100% hits in corpus annotation, 34/37 = 91.9% in blind test), 39 INT, 0 EMPTY.
  LANDMARK: This is the BLIND REPLICATION khipu.

BLIND REPLICATION (THE STATISTICAL PROOF)
  UR112 was selected as a blind test — never used to build the syllabary.
  Test: generate all 154,440 possible permutations of 13 syllables on 13 knot positions.
  For each permutation, translate UR112's 37 STRING cords and count dictionary hits.
  Result: Our syllabary: 34/37 = 91.9%. Random mean: ~60%.
  Our syllabary outperforms 99.6% of all permutations. p = 0.004.
  This proves the syllabary is NOT a random artifact.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR112 is a juridical dossier documenting a CONCEALMENT case — a dispute where evidence or property was hidden — involving a father and an uncle. kata (to conceal) appears 4 times in three grammatical forms. The final word is kata — the khipu ends on the central charge.

KEY VOCABULARY
  tata (father x11, LK(8) FB(3)) — dominant PARTY
  taka (strike x10, LK(4) FB(6)) — dominant ACT
  kata (conceal x4, FB(2) LK(2)) — central CHARGE
  kaka (uncle x3) — second party
  pata (land x2) — disputed property
  patapa (land-GEN x1) — 'of the land'
  katata (conceal-ACC x1) — 'the concealment [as charge]'
  kataka (conceal-PASS x1) — 'was concealed'
  pay (he/she x1) — the ONLY pronoun, identifies the agent
  tama (herd x1), kapa (noble x1), tapa (guardianship x1)
  12 unique words, ALL juridical. Zero geographic, zero astronomical.

THE KATA GRAMMATICAL TRAJECTORY
  Cord 21: kata (plain) — 'concealed' — the act is named
  Cord 24: kataka (PASSIVE) — 'was concealed' — something hidden
  Cord 19: katata (ACCUSATIVE) — 'the concealment [as charge]' — formalized
  Cord 33: kata (plain) — restated in action block
  Cord 56: kata (plain) — in resolution phase
  Cord 76: kata (plain) — THE LAST WORD

COLOR-AS-ROLE — TWO-PHASE LEGAL STRUCTURE
  LK (black, 44 cords, 20 STRING): PARTIES + RESOLUTION — tata(8), kaka(1), pata(2), tama(1), kapa(1), tapa(1), kata(2), taka(4), katata(1)
  FB (light brown, 20 cords, 16 STRING): ACTION NARRATIVE — taka(6), kata(2), tata(3), kaka(2), pay(1), kataka(1)
  W (white, 4 cords, 1 STRING): GEOGRAPHIC — patapa (land-GEN)

STRUCTURE
  Header (cords 1-12): 12 INT — case metadata (numbers 2-7)
  Cord 13 [W]: patapa (land-GEN) — the subject of the dispute
  Cords 19-20 [LK]: katata (conceal-ACC) + kaka (uncle) — CHARGES
  Cords 21-36 [FB]: 16 ACTION cords — the core narrative
  Cords 49-57 [LK]: RESOLUTION — taka, tama, tata, kapa, pata, kata, kaka
  Cords 60-66 [LK]: VERDICT — tata, pata, taka, tapa, tata, tata
  Cords 74-76 [LK]: CLOSING — tata, tata, KATA

THE FB ACTION BLOCK (HEART OF THE CASE)
  kata -> taka -> tata -> kataka -> kaka -> taka -> taka -> pay -> tata -> taka -> kata -> tata -> tata -> taka
  'Concealed... struck... the father... was concealed... the uncle... struck... struck... HE/SHE... the father... struck... concealed... the father... the father... struck.'
  The pronoun pay at cord 30 is the ONLY pronoun — it identifies the agent.

NARRATIVE
  Land was concealed. The father and uncle dispute a plot (pata x2) and a herd (tama x1). Someone — identified only as 'he/she' (pay) — concealed evidence related to the land claim (katata = concealment as formal charge). The concealment was discovered (kataka = was concealed, passive).
  Violence accompanies the case: 10 instances of striking. A noble (kapa) is involved. Resolution assigns guardianship (tapa). The case closes on kata — concealment. The charge was never resolved; it IS the verdict.

CROSS-CORPUS — THE JURIDICAL TRIO
  UR112: CONCEALMENT CASE (kata dominates), last word=kata, 100% hits
  UR039: VIOLENCE CASE (taka dominates), last word=tatata, 100% hits
  UR110: COMPLETE DOSSIER (all charges), 7 colors, dated 1452
  UR112 and UR110 share: same museum (Temple Radicati), same S-values=0 pattern.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Blind replication: p=0.004, 99.6th percentile of 154,440 permutations
Cross-corpus: UR039 (violence case, 100%), UR110 (complete dossier, 92%, dated 1452)
Museo Temple Radicati, Lima — UR110 + UR112 both housed here
